Item 1 source URL is musically.com (same publisher as music-ally.com — the allowed domain). djmag.com ×2, musically.com ×1, musictech.com ×1, cdm.link ×1.
_notes.movies: 5 candidates. Items 4 and 5 both cover Annecy 2026 — the brief already published 2 Annecy stories yesterday (screendaily.com, animationmagazine.net). Curator may want to skip further Annecy coverage. deadline.com ×2, variety.com ×2, postperspective.com ×1.
_notes.games: 8 candidates (rich pool for curator). Content-cluster: items 1, 2, 5, 6 all cover UE6 AI announcement from different angles/outlets — curator should pick 1–2. Items 3 and 7 both cover Steam AI disclosure trend. Item 8 (anime/manga AI localization subsidy) is the freshest angle. Already-published UE6 reactions (80.lv) and Steam/Epic disclosure (wccftech.com) are different URLs from these candidates.
_notes.books: 5 candidates. Items 2 and 3 both cover Beijing International Book Fair 2026 — a third publishingperspectives.com Beijing story was already published yesterday; curator may skip one or both. Scholarly Kitchen ×2 (both on AI provenance/citation infrastructure — distinct subtopics).

design.title: "AI might end up making human creativity more important": 5 questions with Ollie Patterson
design.excerpt: Creative director Ollie Patterson argues that when everyone has access to the same AI tools, human judgment, taste, and strategic thinking become the real competitive edge — AI accelerates the process but cannot set the creative direction.

design.title: A2UI under the hood: Designing for the new era of radically adaptive UI
design.excerpt: Christine Vallaure unpacks the A2UI protocol — a shared language between AI and UI — where interfaces are assembled fresh per user request from a curated catalogue of designer-crafted, accessibility-tested components, making UI radically adaptive without sacrificing quality or consistency.

design.title: Rethinking Figma in an AI World
design.excerpt: Darren Yeo analyses how AI is pulling creative and production work into code editors and agentic environments post-Config 2026, questioning whether Figma's canvas-first approach can remain the centre of gravity as code layers, motion, and shader tools expand what's possible on the canvas.
design._note: Date corrected to 2026-06-27T05:00:00Z (verified in yesterday's collection run; workflow agent initially returned 2026-06-25 which was stale).

design.title: What does Adobe's Topaz Labs acquisition mean for creatives?
design.excerpt: Adobe's planned acquisition of Topaz Labs will bring Topaz's Neurostream AI models for image upscaling, denoising, and enhancement directly into Photoshop, Lightroom, Firefly, and Creative Cloud apps, fundamentally changing on-device AI editing workflows for creatives.

design.title: How to build a landing page using GPT-5 Mini + MUI with UXPin Merge
design.excerpt: A step-by-step workflow tutorial shows how designers can use GPT-5 Mini with the MUI React component library inside UXPin Merge to generate production-ready landing page UI from natural language prompts — without writing raw code.
design._note: Off-list domain (uxpin.com). Included under off-list exception: article is genuinely design×AI relevant and tokens category on-list sources (parallelhq.com, mindstudio.ai, smashingmagazine.com, css-tricks.com, dev.to) had no verifiable fresh items within the 72h window.

music.title: The Atlantic uncovers 'millions' of songs used for AI training
music.excerpt: Journalist Alex Reisner's AI Watchdog project published four searchable databases containing over 21 million songs — scraped largely from YouTube and Spotify — being shared among AI developers to train music-generation models. The investigation lets musicians check for the first time whether their own recordings appear in the datasets behind tools like Suno and Udio.
music._note: Published at musically.com (same publisher as music-ally.com in allowed_domains). Content-cluster: Atlantic AI Watchdog investigation.

music.title: Traxsource to label music as human-made or AI-assisted across platform
music.excerpt: Electronic music store Traxsource will add 'Human-Made' and 'AI-Assisted' labels to every release from 1 July 2026, partnering with AI-detection firms SH Labs and SoundPatrol to classify its entire catalogue while fully AI-generated music will be removed. The move makes Traxsource one of the first retailers to embed AI transparency at the track level.

music.title: Deezer's new Remix Lab feature is AI-free and 'compensates artists accordingly'
music.excerpt: Deezer launched Remix Lab in France, giving fans in-app tools to remix licensed tracks — adjusting tempo, adding reverb, or reshaping genre — without any generative AI involved, while every stream of a fan remix pays royalties to the original artist. The deliberate choice to keep AI out of the remix pipeline is a direct statement against AI-generated content flooding streaming platforms.

music.title: Over 21 million copyrighted songs are circulating among AI developers, watchdog tool launched
music.excerpt: DJ Mag reports on The Atlantic's AI Watchdog investigation revealing four major datasets — collectively holding over 21 million tracks by artists including Björk, Daft Punk, and SZA — being shared freely among AI developers building music-generation systems. The accompanying search tool lets any musician discover within seconds if their discography appears in the training pipeline.
music._note: Content-cluster: Atlantic AI Watchdog investigation (same story as Music Ally item above, DJ Mag angle). djmag.com ×2 in this section.

music.title: Music in training sets is the new Spotify Wrapped: how genAI uses your music
music.excerpt: CDM's Peter Kirn argues that discovering your songs in an AI training dataset has become as routine — and as personally charged — as finding your Spotify Wrapped stats, as The Atlantic's AI Watchdog search tool makes it trivial for any artist to audit which commercial models were fed their catalogue. The piece frames the transparency moment as a turning point for creator consent and compensation.
music._note: Content-cluster: Atlantic AI Watchdog investigation — opinion/framing piece with distinct angle.

movies.title: Is AI Hollywood's New Cosmetic Surgery?
movies.excerpt: Nearly 80% of AI work in Hollywood goes unnoticed as studios secretly deploy generative tools for de-aging, VFX fixes, and post-production on major releases, terrified that public acknowledgment will spark audience backlash. An influential AI technologist's company is credited on only a fifth of the projects it completes, even as the tech proves sophisticated enough to pass undetected.

movies.title: Cascade Studio Launches AI-Native Platform for Storytelling
movies.excerpt: Hollywood VFX veterans behind Avengers: Endgame, Wicked, and No Time to Die have launched Cascade in open beta — an agentic AI production platform designed to give solo creators and small teams access to studio-grade storytelling infrastructure, keeping creative decisions in human hands while automating asset management and workflow orchestration.

movies.title: Former Syfy Executive Mark Stern, Lloyd Braun Launch AI Animation Studio Intelligent Animation
movies.excerpt: Veteran TV executives Mark Stern and Lloyd Braun have launched Intelligent Animation, a studio that fuses human-written scripts, human voice actors, and hand-drawn art with machine-learning AI tools to produce animated shows faster and cheaper than traditional pipelines. The studio's first two microseries debut later this year on Braun's aTwist platform.

movies.title: Annecy's MIFA Bets on Cross-IP, AI and Global Growth Amid Animation Financing Squeeze
movies.excerpt: At the 2026 Annecy MIFA market, AI has moved from a flashpoint of protest to a grudgingly accepted industry fixture, with closed-door workshops, an AI4Animation think tank, and ethical AI partnerships dominating the agenda. Deals such as Banijay's arrangement with Toon Boom to test AI tools on kids' content signal the animation industry's cautious but accelerating embrace of the technology.
movies._note: Annecy content — brief already published screendaily.com AI4Animation and animationmagazine.net French animators stories yesterday. Curator may deprioritise further Annecy coverage.

movies.title: 5 Talking Points Ahead Of Annecy: 'Minions', AI, IP, Studios, China
movies.excerpt: AI dominated the pre-show conversation at Annecy 2026 as studios and independent producers grappled with how to discuss generative tools without alienating artists. From ethical AI partnerships with animation software firms to new discussions about whether AI can replace or augment traditional hand-drawn workflows, the festival's agenda reflected an industry at a crossroads.
movies._note: Annecy content — pre-festival preview. deadline.com ×2 in this section.

games.title: Unreal Engine 6 will merge UE5 and UEFN into one engine
games.excerpt: Epic Games announced Unreal Engine 6 at Unreal Fest Chicago on June 17, 2026, revealing that UE5 and the Fortnite editor (UEFN) will unify into one engine packed with generative AI pipeline features. LLMs including Claude, Gemini, and Codex will play a 'central role' in helping developers build content faster through an MCP-based AI integration layer.
games._note: UE6 cluster: primary announcement. Curator should pick 1–2 from items 1, 2, 5, 6 (all UE6-related).

games.title: The Next Version Of Unreal Engine Is Going To Be Filled With GenAI Tools
games.excerpt: Kotaku reports on Epic's Unreal Engine 6 announcement, noting the next-gen engine will integrate LLMs and generative AI models such as Claude and Codex as first-class creative and productivity tools for game development. Engine development lead Marcus Wassmer confirmed UE6's AI tools will let developers build content faster while retaining creative control.

games.title: Even more games on Steam are using AI: nearly 1,700 of 8,600 Next Fest demos use AI
games.excerpt: GamesRadar counted 1,694 of the 8,682 demos in Steam Next Fest June 2026 carry an AI content disclosure, meaning roughly one in five upcoming games openly uses generative AI in some form. The surge highlights how AI-assisted game creation has gone from a novelty to a near-mainstream practice on the storefront.
games._note: Steam AI disclosure cluster: see also pcgamer.com item below (weekly Steam review, same theme).

games.title: Kingdom Hearts Collection cover art quietly changes following accusations of AI generation
games.excerpt: Square Enix silently swapped the overseas Kingdom Hearts Collection box art after fans flagged telltale AI-generation artefacts — unusual fingers on Donald Duck, warped shoe geometry on Sora — with no public statement from the company. Retailers began receiving the uncontroversial Japanese cover variant as a replacement.

games.title: Epic Games Loses Level Design Legend Sjoerd de Jong After 12 Years As Unreal Engine 6 Embraces AI
games.excerpt: Veteran Unreal Engine lead evangelist Sjoerd de Jong has left Epic Games after 12 years, with his departure timed to coincide almost exactly with the Unreal Engine 6 announcement that positions Claude, Gemini, and Codex as central to the new development pipeline. De Jong acknowledged the industry is at a 'pivotal point' requiring adaptation.
games._note: UE6 cluster: human-interest angle on AI's creative toll.

games.title: Tim Sweeney Admits Epic's Unreal Engine AI Tools Risk 'AI slop', but Calls them an Accelerant for Real Creators
games.excerpt: Epic CEO Tim Sweeney acknowledged in an IGN interview that Unreal Engine's new generative AI tools will inevitably produce low-quality 'AI slop,' but argued that in the hands of professional and serious indie developers these tools act as a powerful accelerant rather than a replacement for human creativity. Sweeney also warned developers against over-reliance on AI due to rising token costs.
games._note: UE6 cluster: CEO perspective. wccftech.com ×2 in this section.

games.title: Steam Week in Review: More than 300 games released last week, 120 had AI disclosures
games.excerpt: PC Gamer's weekly Steam analysis found that 120 of the 338 games released in the week ending June 14, 2026 carried an AI disclosure, with many using generative AI not just for translations but for artwork, sound, and narrative assets. The data underlines how rapidly AI-assisted game production has scaled on Steam's storefront.
games._note: Steam AI disclosure cluster: similar theme to GamesRadar Next Fest item.

games.title: Anime Publishers Reportedly Encouraged To Use AI Translations
games.excerpt: Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI) is reportedly preparing an 11.5 billion yen (~$70 million) subsidy program targeting nine anime, manga, and gaming publishers — including Square Enix, Bandai Namco, and Crunchyroll — to incentivize the adoption of generative AI for official translations. The program aims to accelerate overseas localisation and undercut pirated translations.

books.title: Bestselling Nonfiction Author Believes AI Has Devastated His Book Sales
books.excerpt: Tim Ferriss reports his catalog will sell roughly 80% fewer print copies in 2026 than in 2022, with his agent noting '2025 was the first big drop, 2026 looks more severe, and the only thing that's really changed in that timeframe is the acceleration of AI.' Jane Friedman examines the data as a bellwether for how-to and self-help publishing.

books.title: Beijing International Book Fair Highlights Digital Publishing, Cross-Media Adaptations
books.excerpt: At the 40th edition of the Beijing International Book Fair, AI and publishing transformation dominated forums, with a new Internet Publishing Joint Pavilion showcasing AI-generated content tools and workflow applications from 20 exhibitors including provincial publishing groups and university presses. The fair drew 1,700+ exhibitors from 82 countries and highlighted how Chinese digital publishing is reshaping the global industry.
books._note: Beijing book fair cluster: brief already published publishingperspectives.com Beijing story yesterday — curator may deprioritise.

books.title: Artificial Intelligence Already Embedded in Publishing in China, Beijing International Book Fair Hears
books.excerpt: AI dominated discussions at the Beijing International Book Fair's 40th edition, with organizers and keynote speakers presenting AI as a practical tool already woven into Chinese publishers' daily workflows — from AI-generated content creation to automated translation and editorial support. Unlike Western markets still debating AI's role, Chinese publishers showcased live deployments, signaling a global split.
books._note: Beijing book fair cluster: different angle (East vs. West contrast). thebookseller.com URL is unique vs. published history.

books.title: Attribution, Provenance, Reference, Citation, and AI for Research Applications – Understanding the Differences
books.excerpt: As AI retrieval systems decompose scholarly books and articles into semantic chunks and vector embeddings, provenance and citation information no longer automatically survive the process — creating a trust crisis for academic publishing. This analysis unpacks why attribution, provenance, reference, and citation each demand distinct technical solutions as AI reshapes how researchers manage the scholarly record.

books.title: Making AI Use of Scholarly Content Traceable, Measurable, and Trustworthy: A Meeting Report from Cambridge Scholarly AI Workshop
books.excerpt: A Cambridge workshop convened by scholarly publishers proposed new infrastructure standards to keep book and journal content traceable, attributable, and measurable as generative AI systems ingest and redistribute it — addressing how subscription and open-access content alike loses its source identity when processed by RAG systems and agentic AI tools.
books._note: scholarlykitchen.sspnet.org ×2. Distinct subtopics: item 4 = conceptual taxonomy; item 5 = workshop/standards proposal.
